title: Service Outcomes, Boundaries, and Server Lifecycle · Runtime Contract: Service Outcome Contract
summary: A civic incident API has endpoints but no stated user outcome, ownership boundary, startup contract, dependency readiness, shutdown policy, or evidence connecting a request to durable effect. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo service outcome contract increment in service outcome, boundary, and lifecycle map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define a server outcome with user, client, operator, security, and accessibility evidence instead of treating endpoint count as success.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Service Outcome Contract · Theory Service Outcome Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 6206 in a internal administrative API crossing two trust zones. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate listener liveness, dependency readiness, and draining state; then change the tenant and show deny-by-default before repository access. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define a server outcome with user, client, operator, security, and accessibility evidence instead of treating endpoint count as success.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Service Outcome Contract contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Go server Predict Service Outcome Contract · Theory Service Outcome Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define a server outcome with user, client, operator, security, and accessibility evidence instead of treating endpoint count as success. For Go server Predict Service Outcome Contract · Theory Service Outcome Contract, keep Service Outcome Contract connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name method, authority, route, identity, resource budgets, handler and dependency ownership, response effect, cleanup, and recovery; run a pure deterministic Go decision model; then change one request or failure and state the listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, or production transfer gate.
